question_text: Which of the following statements is/are TRUE with respect to deadlocks?

options.A: Circular wait is a necessary condition for the formation of deadlock.
options.B: In a system where each resource has more than one instance, a cycle in its wait- for graph indicates the presence of a deadlock.
options.C: If the current allocation of resources to processes leads the system to unsafe state, then deadlock will necessarily occur.
options.D: In the resource-allocation graph of a system, if every edge is an assignment edge, then the system is not in deadlock state.
